node.title: UBER | Phone Screen | L5A | San Francisco

topic.title: UBER | Phone Screen | L5A | San Francisco

post.content:
Question asked for Uber Java Software Engineering Position(Phone Interview): 

**Convert json (with constraints) to csv**

Input

```
[
{
			"f1": "v1",
			"f2": [{"f3": "v2"}, "v3"],
			"f4": {"f5": ["v4", {"f6": "v5"}], "f7": "v6"},
            "f8": ["v7", "v8", "v9", ["v10"]]

	},
{
			"f1": "a1",
			"f2": [{"f3": "a2"}, "a3"],
			"f4": {"f5": ["a4", {"f6": "a5"}], "f7": "a6"},

	},
]
```

Output:

```
f1, f2.f3, f2, f4.f5, f4.f5.f6, f4.f7
v1, v2, v3, v4, v5, v6
a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6
```
...



Constraints

* The key names are the same in all nested dictionaries.
* The values are different across dictionaries.
* The keys can be in any order in the output as long as the corresponding values are mapped to the keys.
* There is no limit to the nesting depth and there can be any arbitrary combinations of lists, dictionaries and strings.
Is this without using any 3rd party libraries? or do we need to parse all the char to detect array/obj?
So did this happen instead of the OA or after the OA ? Were your OA questions (if you gave one) LC\'s Uber tagged?\nAny tips on what topics they were on?
